message:
feat(extensions,pijs_workload): native-only runtime engine selection and indexed extension dispatch

Remove QuickJS from ExtensionRuntimeEngineSelection enum, making NativeRust
the sole production runtime engine. The from_env_value() constructor now
unconditionally returns NativeRust regardless of input, reflecting the
project's migration away from QuickJS for production extension execution.

In NativeRustRuntimeState, replace linear O(n) extension lookups with
HashMap-based indexed dispatch for tools, commands, shortcuts, provider
streams, and event hooks. Add rebuild_indexes() to construct these indexes
on extension load, and convert find_tool_extension(), find_command_extension(),
find_shortcut_extension(), and dispatch_event() to use O(1) index lookups
instead of iterating all extensions. Add new provider_stream_chunks() and
reset_transient_state() helpers, and a load_extensions() method that
coordinates extension loading with index rebuilding.

In pijs_workload benchmark binary, add a third NativeRustRuntime lane that
exercises the real ExtensionRuntimeHandle path (not just the synthetic
preview path), including writing a .native.json descriptor, starting the
NativeRustExtensionRuntimeHandle, loading via ExtensionManager, and
executing tool calls through the full runtime pipeline. Fix floating-point
per-call timing precision by using f64::from(u32) conversion instead of
casting u128 directly to f64. Add proper shutdown cleanup and a new
native_runtime_handle_roundtrip_resolves test.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
